_meta.purpose: Typed interface registry for all agent-callable tools. Agents load this before deciding what to call. Marina Wyss AI Agents Course (15:51): 'Every tool has two parts: the interface for the agent — a tool name, a plain English description of when to use it, and a typed input schema — and the implementation code. The agent only sees the interface.'
_meta.usage: Read this file at the start of any improvement execution. Select tool by name. Use input_schema to validate args before calling. Check output_format to parse the result correctly. Check error_codes before assuming success.

dispatch.description: Send structured messages between agents and Kai. Use for: reporting completed work, flagging blockers, requesting cross-agent review, escalating failures.
dispatch.when_to_use: After completing any significant work unit. After any failure that requires coordination. Before ending an agent cycle.

agent.description: Agent name (nel, ra, sam, aether, dex)

dispatch.output_format: Text confirmation line. Exit code 0 = success, non-zero = failure.

error_codes.1: dispatch.sh not found or not executable

dispatch.example: bash bin/dispatch.sh report nel 'Completed dependency audit — 3 SPOFs found'

kai_push.description: Publish an event to the HQ feed visible at hyo.world. Use when an agent completes a research file, ships an improvement, or produces output Hyo should see.
kai_push.when_to_use: After completing any output artifact (research file, report, improvement). NOT for internal state changes.

message.description: Event description — what was produced and why it matters

kai_push.output_format: Lines: '==> Pushing to HQ: <agent>', 'http <code>', '✓ pushed to HQ' or error. Note: '(HYO_HQ_PASSWORD not set — skipping verification)' means push succeeded but readback could not be confirmed.

error_codes.http_non_200: HQ API rejected push — check payload format

kai_push.example: HYO_ROOT=~/Documents/Projects/Hyo bash bin/kai.sh push nel 'Dependency audit complete — 3 critical SPOFs identified'

ticket.description: Create a structured improvement ticket in kai/tickets/. Use to track work that spans multiple cycles or requires Kai review.
ticket.when_to_use: When an improvement requires >1 cycle. When a failure needs tracking. When a weakness is identified that requires a systemic fix.
ticket.output_format: Ticket ID (e.g. T-2026-0042). JSONL entry appended to kai/tickets/tickets.jsonl.
ticket.example: bash bin/ticket.sh --title 'Add retry logic to ra pipeline' --type improvement --agent ra --weakness W2

agent_execute_improvement.description: Execute a researched improvement by calling Claude API with the aric-latest.json thesis and generating real code. Run only after ARIC Phases 1-5 are complete.
agent_execute_improvement.when_to_use: When aric-latest.json has a populated improvement_built.thesis and files_to_change. NOT before research is complete.
agent_execute_improvement.output_format: Multiline log with [execute-improvement][<agent>/<id>] prefix. Final block shows EXECUTION ENGINE COMPLETE with status, commit hash, verify result.

error_codes.1: Gate failure — check which GATE number failed in stderr
error_codes.Claude API error: API key missing or quota exceeded
error_codes.verify_failed: Code written and committed but verify.sh failed — requires manual review

agent_research.description: Run ARIC research phases for an agent — web search, source synthesis, weakness identification. Populates aric-latest.json.
agent_research.when_to_use: At the start of each agent improvement cycle. Before calling agent_execute_improvement.
agent_research.output_format: Research file written to agents/<agent>/research/. aric-latest.json updated with research_conducted field.

error_codes.non-zero: Research phase failed — check for API quota or network issues

kai_exec.description: Run a command on the Mini (local machine) via the queue. Use for any operation requiring local file access, git, launchctl, or system tools.
kai_exec.when_to_use: When a command must run on the Mini rather than in a sandbox. For git push, launchctl, brew, or file operations on the actual Hyo project.

command.description: Shell command to run on the Mini

kai_exec.output_format: stdout/stderr of the command as run on the Mini. May include queue worker prefix.

error_codes.queue_down: Queue worker not running — check kai-autonomous.sh status

kai_exec.example: kai exec "cd ~/Documents/Projects/Hyo && git push origin main"

chaos_inject.description: Deliberately remove one agent dependency for ≤5 minutes to test resilience. Discovers SPOFs before production does.
chaos_inject.when_to_use: Scheduled Saturday 05:00 MT. Can be run manually to test specific dependency.
chaos_inject.output_format: PASS/FAIL per dependency tested. SPOF = fallback expected but not used, or silent failure. Opens P1 ticket per SPOF found.

error_codes.SPOF_found: Real fragility — fix before next chaos run
error_codes.timeout: Dependency not restored in 5 min — check agent state

verify_improvement_content_gate.description: Run content-gate check on a completed improvement — verifies the change addresses the specific test case, not just existence of files.
verify_improvement_content_gate.when_to_use: After agent_execute_improvement completes. Before marking a ticket as resolved.
verify_improvement_content_gate.output_format: PASS or FAIL with specific reason. FAIL means the improvement passed file-existence gates but did not solve the actual problem.

error_codes.FAIL: Pipeline specification gaming detected — improvement is superficial

cross_agent_review.description: Run adversarial cross-review between agents (Nel↔Sam) to catch self-serving self-assessments. Addresses sycophancy at 58% baseline.
cross_agent_review.when_to_use: Daily (Mon-Sat) at 06:45 MT. Run manually after any agent produces a self-assessment that seems overly positive.
cross_agent_review.output_format: Review report written to agents/<name>/logs/cross-review-YYYY-MM-DD.md per agent pair.

error_codes.non-zero: Review failed — check API key and network
